summary: No-ship: one tombstone race remains for worktree-backed sessions matched by cwd.

findings.title: Matched hook path can bypass the cwd tombstone and resurrect a deleted worktree session
findings.recommendation: When applyHookEvent updates a cwd-matched record, pass the matched hook cwd/worktreePath into setSessionStatus, or move the match/update decision under one locked critical section. Today a hook can read a worktree row, DELETE /sessions can tombstone the worktreePath and remove it, then the stale hook calls setSessionStatus(projectPath, matchedKey, ..., { claudeSessionId }) without opts.worktreePath. The create path checks isTombstoned(projectPath) instead of the deleted worktree cwd and recreates the row with worktreePath=projectPath. Add a regression for a worktree session where applyHookEvent races deleteSession/removeSessionRecord and assert the tombstoned cwd is not recreated.
